pain_point_label: Helpdesk Ticket Triage Chaos & Slow Resolution

matches.match_rationale: Incoming helpdesk requests (email, chat, SMS) can be classified by urgency, category, and impact — automatically separating critical issues like enrollment blocks or grade entry failures from low-priority requests like printer jams. This directly addresses the core triage pain: the inability to prioritize without manual review of every inbound message.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI triage layer that reads incoming messages from all channels (email, Teams, SMS), assigns priority (critical/high/medium/low) and category (LMS access, enrollment, password reset, hardware), then routes or queues accordingly — before a human ever sees the ticket.

matches.match_rationale: The full lifecycle of helpdesk ticket management — ingesting requests from multiple channels, classifying them, creating structured tickets, routing to the right person, sending acknowledgment, and following up — is a multi-step workflow with branching decisions that a task automation agent can own end-to-end. The current state (no ticket system, requests via email/chat/SMS, follow-ups lost) is exactly the gap this capability fills.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI agent that monitors all inbound channels, extracts ticket details, creates a structured record, assigns priority and owner, sends user acknowledgment with expected SLA, and escalates unresolved tickets after defined thresholds — removing the COO escalation loop entirely.

matches.match_rationale: A significant fraction of helpdesk volume (password resets, LMS access instructions, enrollment status checks) can be resolved without human intervention through a conversational agent that guides users through self-service steps. This directly reduces the ticket volume the single IT person must handle, compressing resolution time from 3-5 days to minutes for routine requests.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A staff-facing chatbot deployed in Teams or a portal that handles the top 5-10 request types autonomously — resetting passwords via API, providing LMS login steps, checking enrollment status — escalating to a human only when it cannot resolve.

matches.match_rationale: Requests arriving via email, chat, and SMS are unstructured and inconsistent. Transforming these free-form messages into structured ticket records (category, affected system, user, campus, timestamp, urgency signals) imposes the schema needed to enable prioritization, tracking, and SLA measurement — which is the foundational gap before any triage logic can work.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI parsing layer that converts raw inbound messages into structured ticket objects with standardized fields, feeding a simple ticketing database or spreadsheet that currently does not exist.
